title: T-012 — Quality Hold consumer service helper for RPT-004 QC Holds Dashboard (12-REPORTING §10.1)
prompt:
# T-012 — Quality Hold consumer service helper

PRD: docs/prd/12-REPORTING-PRD.md §10.1 (QC Holds Dashboard P1), §4.1 #4
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Provide a thin consumer wrapper over the 09-QUALITY-owned `quality_holds` + `hold_items` tables (and the `mv_qc_holds_summary` MV from T-006) so the QC Holds dashboard reads via the consumer pattern (cross-module navigation only, no transactional writes to 09-QA). Cross-module link 'View in QA' resolves to `/quality/holds/<holdId>` route owned by 09-QUALITY.

## Implementation contract
1. Service helper `packages/reporting/src/queries/quality-holds.ts`:
   - `getQcHoldsSummary({orgId, siteId, shift?, line?, dateRange})` reads `mv_qc_holds_summary` (from T-006).
   - `getQcHoldDetail(holdId)` reads `quality_holds` row via 09-QUALITY's read-only contract; returns null if not found; never INSERT/UPDATE.
   - All reads append `WHERE org_id = app.current_org_id() AND (site_id = $current OR site_id IS NULL)`.
   - Cross-module link builder `buildQualityHoldUrl(holdId)` returns `/quality/holds/{holdId}` (validated UUID format).
2. Type definitions `QcHoldSummary`, `QcHoldDetail` derived from PRD §10.1 display column list.
3. Vitest contract test: (a) cross-org leak test on summary path, (b) link builder rejects non-UUID holdId, (c) read-only enforcement — attempting to call any setter on 09-QA tables via this service is a type error, (d) MV-missing path returns empty array + degraded flag (consistent with T-011 pattern).

## Files
- packages/reporting/src/queries/quality-holds.ts [create]
- packages/reporting/src/types/quality-holds.ts [create]
- packages/reporting/__tests__/quality-holds-consumer.test.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given two orgs have seeded quality_holds + hold_items rows, when getQcHoldsSummary is called under each org session, then cross-org leak test passes (zero leaks).
2. Given buildQualityHoldUrl is called with a non-UUID string, when invoked, then it throws InvalidHoldIdError; with a valid UUID, then it returns `/quality/holds/<uuid>`.
3. Given the TypeScript service surface is inspected, when only `get*` exports are listed (no setters), then any attempt to add an INSERT / UPDATE / DELETE method fails project ESLint rule (consumer-pattern guard).
4. Given mv_qc_holds_summary is absent (e.g., not yet refreshed), when getQcHoldsSummary is called, then it returns `{available: false, data: [], reason: 'mv_not_refreshed'}` — graceful degradation consistent with T-011 pattern.
5. Given a filter on shift=AM is passed, when query runs, then the WHERE clause includes `am_pm = 'AM'` exactly and rows are scoped accordingly.

## Test strategy (RED first)
- RED: `packages/reporting/__tests__/quality-holds-consumer.test.ts` covers 5 ACs with testcontainers Postgres + seeded 09-QA rows.
- Run `pnpm --filter @monopilot/reporting test quality-holds-consumer`.
- GREEN: write service helper + types.

## Risk red lines
- Do not duplicate quality_holds workflow logic — consumer pattern only (read-only).
- Do not INSERT/UPDATE on 09-QA tables from reporting service surface; ESLint rule blocks setters.
- Do not bypass app.current_org_id() — RLS-via-service.
- Do not let cross-module link builder accept raw strings — UUID validation only.
- Do not couple to 09-QA internal column names beyond the published consumer contract (see 09-QUALITY coverage.md or PRD §6.1 if coverage absent).
- Do not make this helper a write side; Production WO hold gate (CIG-3 in Auditor A audit) is 08-PROD/09-QA's job, not reporting.
